description: Wire architectural-health interrogation into the improver's detector surface. The detector rows already exist in hex-cli/assets/improver/detectors.toml as scaffolding; this workplan ships the underlying CLI flags + STDB rollups they call. After this, the improver's tick walks the hexagon end-to-end (domain → port → adapter), surfaces design and operational findings as hypotheses, and the existing P2/P3 pipeline (adversarial variants + judge) routes them to ADR + workplan drafts.
feature: Architectural-health detectors — domain → port → adapter interrogation

phases.description: Static design detector flags on `hex analyze`. AST-only, no runtime telemetry. Each --flag produces JSON the improver can parse.

gate.command: cargo test -p hex-analyzer architectural_detectors && hex analyze . --port-cohesion --json | jq '.findings | length'

phases.name: hex analyze — static-design flags

tasks.description: Add `--orphan-adapters` and `--orphan-ports` flags to hex analyze. Implementation: walk all `impl Port for Adapter` blocks via tree-sitter, build a set of (port_id, adapter_id) pairs; cross-reference against composition-root binding map. orphan-adapters = bound nowhere; orphan-ports = no adapter impls anywhere. Each emits JSON `{findings: [{kind, port, adapter?, file, line}]}`. Existing `hex analyze . --json` schema gains a new top-level array per kind. Tests in hex-analyzer's existing test harness against fixture workspaces.
tasks.name: Orphan-adapter / orphan-port detectors

tasks.description: Add `--port-cohesion` flag. Implementation: for each Port trait, count public methods, cluster by name-prefix and parameter-type overlap, emit a finding when method count > 7 OR cluster count >= 2 with low cross-cluster similarity. Threshold + cluster algorithm declared as constants in the source — keep the heuristic legible. JSON output: `{findings: [{kind: 'port_cohesion', port, file, line, method_count, clusters: [[...], [...]]}]}`. Tests against fixtures: a single-concern port with 4 methods should not flag; a kitchen-sink port with 12 methods across 3 concerns should flag with all 3 clusters listed.
tasks.name: Port-cohesion heuristic detector

tasks.description: Add `--god-types --json`. Walks domain/ tree-sitter and flags any struct/enum with >300 LOC or >10 public methods. Configurable thresholds via `[[analyzer.god_type]]` in `.hex/project.json`. Output: `{findings: [{kind, type, file, lines, public_methods}]}`.
tasks.name: God-domain-type detector

tasks.description: Add `--adapter-duplication --json` and `--dead-layers --json`. Adapter-duplication uses tree-sitter token similarity (Jaccard on AST node bag) across adapter implementations of any pair sharing a port; threshold 0.6. Dead-layers walks the call graph from primary adapters and emits a finding for any layer dir with zero inbound edges. Output schemas mirror the others.
tasks.name: Adapter-duplication + dead-layer detectors

tasks.description: Add `--composition-churn --window=<duration>` flag. Implementation: walk `git log --since=<window> -- '*composition-root*' '*compose*.rs' '*lib.rs'` and count distinct commits touching wiring; cross-reference with ADRs accepted in the same window. Emits a finding when churn / accepted-adr-count > 1.5. Output: `{findings: [{commits_touching_wiring, accepted_adrs_in_window, ratio, files_touched}]}`.
tasks.name: Composition-drift detector

phases.description: Operational telemetry rollup. The substrate's PortTelemetry trait (commit 5556a65f) collects per-call metrics. The improver needs aggregate views.

gate.command: cargo test -p hex-nexus port_telemetry::rollup && hex substrate telemetry --port-latency --window=7d --json

phases.name: hex substrate telemetry — runtime rollups

tasks.description: Add a daily rollup job in hex-nexus/src/sched_service.rs (sibling to spawn_substrate_autopilot) that reads raw PortTelemetry events from the last 24h and writes a `port_telemetry_daily` STDB row per (port_id, adapter_id) with: call_count, p50, p99, success_rate, error_kinds_top3. 7-day rollup similarly. Reducer in spacetime-modules/hexflo-coordination/. Test: insert 1000 synthetic events, run rollup, assert one row per (port, adapter) with correct percentiles.
tasks.name: PortTelemetry daily/weekly rollup

tasks.description: New CLI subcommand tree `hex substrate telemetry` with flags `--port-latency`, `--adapter-skew`, `--traffic-concentration`, `--adapter-idle`, `--window=<duration>`, all with `--json`. Each reads the rollup STDB rows from P2.1 and emits a `{findings: [...]}` envelope. Drift-detection logic: latency uses week-over-week delta >25%, skew uses 2x ratio between any two adapters on same port, concentration is single-port share >0.7, idle is zero calls in window. MCP parity per ADR-019.
tasks.name: hex substrate telemetry CLI + MCP

tasks.description: Add `hex substrate swaps --since=<duration> --json`. Reads swap_ticket STDB table and emits findings when promoted-count over the window is below threshold (default: 0). Output: `{findings: [{kind: 'substrate_swap_starvation', window, promoted_count}]}`. Note: this detector flags an absence — emitting a finding when there's nothing to report — so it's the inverse of the others.
tasks.name: hex substrate swaps detector

phases.description: End-to-end proof: a fixture workspace exercises every detector class and the improver picks at least one architectural hypothesis per class.
phases.name: E2E + judge regression

tasks.description: Build a fixture workspace under hex-cli/tests/fixtures/architectural-health/ containing: (a) a god-domain-type (350 LOC, 12 methods); (b) an orphan adapter (impl block but no composition-root binding); (c) a kitchen-sink port (10 methods, 3 prefix clusters); (d) two adapters duplicating logic; (e) synthetic PortTelemetry rows showing latency drift and adapter skew. Test: run `hex sched improver discover --once --json --repo=<fixture>`; assert ≥1 hypothesis from class A (static), ≥1 from class B (operational), ≥1 from class C (composition). Then run the judge against those hypotheses and assert each gets a winner with confidence >=0.6 OR a RejectAll with `human_review_requested`.
tasks.name: Fixture workspace + improver e2e

tasks.description: Run the new detectors against THIS workspace (hex-intf itself, dogfooding per ADR-008). Capture output to docs/analysis/architectural-health-baseline-2026-04-27.json. Operator reviews by hand once before this lands; the file becomes the regression baseline so future runs can flag deltas. No code change in this task — just running the analyzer and committing the JSON snapshot.
tasks.name: Dogfood baseline against hex-intf
